name: Adhana Tara (19th Star) — Geographical Relocation Trigger
description: The transit of planets over the 19th Nakshatra from the birth star acts as a specialized spatial trigger for geographical relocation and establishing a new residence.

source_text.work_name: Nakshatras as Celestial Blueprints: An Analytical Study of Lunar Mansions in Predicting Major Life Events
source_text.quote: Adhana 19th Star... Triggers geographical relocation, career settlement, and creative output.

all_of: Transiting planets pass over the exact longitude of the 19th Nakshatra (Adhana Tara) counted from the natal Janma Nakshatra

interpretation.meaning: The transit of planets over the 19th Nakshatra from the birth star acts as a specialized spatial trigger for geographical relocation, establishing a new residence, and settling in a new location.

metadata.notes: A longitude-sensitive transit activation technique based on the Navatara (Tara Bala) and broader Nakshatra counting systems.

name: Desha Tara (27th Star) — Homeland Alienation Trigger
description: Malefic transits over the 27th star from the natal Moon disrupt the native's relationship with their homeland, leading to alienation and migration.

source_text.work_name: Nakshatras as Celestial Blueprints: An Analytical Study of Lunar Mansions in Predicting Major Life Events
source_text.quote: Desha 27th Star... Determines relationship to the homeland; malefic transits can alienate the native from their community.

all_of: Transiting malefic planets pass over the exact longitude of the 27th Nakshatra (Desha Tara) counted from the natal Janma Nakshatra

interpretation.meaning: Malefic transits (such as Saturn, Rahu, or Ketu) over the 27th star from the natal Moon disrupt the native's relationship with their homeland, leading to alienation from their local community and separation from their roots via migration.

metadata.notes: Specifies a challenging trigger for leaving one's homeland, often under difficult or alienating circumstances.

name: Saturn/Rahu/Ketu Transit 6th-8th Nakshatra (Right Leg) — Physical Travel
description: When slow-moving karmic planets transit the 6th-8th Nakshatras from the birth star (symbolizing the right leg), it activates a physical window for travel and movement.

source_text.quote: The transits of Saturn, Raha and Ketu - Distribution amongst Limbs Effects ... (c) 6th, 7th, 8th - right leg travel

any_of: Transit Saturn passes through the 6th, 7th, or 8th Nakshatra counted from the natal Janma Nakshatra
Transit Rahu passes through the 6th, 7th, or 8th Nakshatra counted from the natal Janma Nakshatra
Transit Ketu passes through the 6th, 7th, or 8th Nakshatra counted from the natal Janma Nakshatra

interpretation.meaning: When slow-moving karmic planets (Saturn, Rahu, or Ketu) transit the 6th, 7th, or 8th Nakshatras from the birth star—which symbolize the right leg of the Kala Purusha—it activates a physical window for travel and movement away from the current residence.

metadata.notes: A classic limb-based Nakshatra transit rule (Anga Gochara) pinpointing extended periods of travel triggered by slow-moving outer planets.
